mechanism_narrative: Higher real short-term interest rates reflect expanding consumer credit demand and economic activity, which increases equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in >55% of periods when RSAFS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 0.5%, monetary policy tightens sufficiently to compress equity discount rates and increase borrowing costs, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in the unemployment rate and expansion in total nonfarm payrolls confirms robust labor market absorption, which raises consumer spending forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when UNRATE falls but PAYEMS stagnates, or combined Rank IC drops below 0.03 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above its long-term trend signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 60-day SPX returns in >50% of observations, or the mechanism reverses direction during inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, which improves corporate revenue projections and triggers immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of triggered quarters, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stagnant unemployment rate indicates stagflationary pressure, which forces tighter monetary policy expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal predicts positive returns when CPIAUCSL rises and UNRATE holds steady, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts alongside contracting industrial production signal a synchronized downturn in construction and manufacturing demand, which reduces equity valuations and causes negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns remain positive in >60% of synchronized downturn periods, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation exceeding 0.3% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >55% of high-inflation months, or the threshold loses predictive power when inflation is driven by transitory supply shocks.
